title: rubellia aurea
intro: Rubellia aurea, commonly known as Golden Ragwort, is a flowering plant species belonging to the aster family. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering several health benefits such as reducing inflammation and pain, as well as aiding in the treatment of respiratory issues like bronchitis and asthma. The therapeutic actions of Rubellia aurea are attributed to its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory, expectorant, and antispasmodic, making it a potential remedy for various health conditions. Bioactive constituents present in the plant include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Rubellia aurea can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and increased heart rate. In severe cases, improper use of the plant can also lead to liver damage, nausea, and diarrhea. Additionally, using Rubellia aurea excessively can cause headaches, fatigue, and even interact negatively with certain medications. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using the plant to avoid these potential side effects.
